learning_outcomes.description: Use a project brief from industry or a research lab to identify a problem, its associated risks and opportunities, and to formulate a solution.

Locate and evaluate relevant literature and prior art; locate, access, select and use technical and commercial information from various sources; relate prior knowledge to the problem to identify how it can best be applied to tackle the problem.
Gather and analyse data or elicit requirements, and develop concepts.
Select and apply appropriate research or design methods and be able to draw conclusions based on evidence and argument.

Maintain professional standard records; use the means provided on a regular basis to create a thorough record of ideas, plans, discussion, decisions etc. and to reflect on progress made and milestones reached.
Use appropriate conventions to communicate the information in reports and presentations.
Identify critical aspects of professional behaviour within the professional context and conduct yourself accordingly.

assessments.assessment_title: Pre-placement modules and UQ Academic Integrity Tutorial
assessments.due_date:
22/06/2026 - 28/07/2026

Please complete Pre-placement modules BEFORE starting placement.
assessments.task_description:
Students are required to complete the following "Pre-placement modules" prior to starting placement:

  1. Placement Expectation Module
  2. Engineering Professional Practice Module 1
  3. Engineering Professional Practice Module 2

See links on Blackboard.

Academic integrity is particularly relevant to a thesis-style project so we encourage students to complete this tutorial early in the placement.

You can access the tutorial online here: https://www.uq.edu.au/integrity/ (you must copy and paste this link into your browser to access the tutorial).
assessments.submission_guidelines: Completion should be automatically recorded. However, please keep record of completion to provide if requested.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Students must complete the Placement Commencement Check. The assessment is designed to identify placement issues early and confirm that the student has commenced the placement satisfactorily and is meeting professional expectations.

This is a hurdle assessment requirement.

Where concerns are identified, students must successfully participate in the Placement Review process and complete any required remediation or improvement actions. Failure to satisfactorily address concerns identified through the Placement Commencement Check may result in withdrawal from the placement or failure in the course.


Student submission (via Blackboard):

This may include a brief document with the following:

  • Placement details and supervisor information (Workplace and academic supervisors).
  • Confirmation of attendance and commencement date.
  • Confirmation that inductions and required training have been completed or planned (Provide list of those conducted and not yet conducted)
  • Brief summary of role, project brief and key activities (100-300 words).
  • Brief reflection on initial expectations and goals (100-300 words).
  • Provide any additional comments, including any issues or concerns.

Supervisor confirmation.

Workplace and academic supervisors are encouraged to notify the Course Coordinator as soon as significant concerns arise and should not wait for the Placement Commencement Check or subsequent Workplace Feedback assessments.

This checkpoint will include, for example, completion of a short online form by the workplace supervisor to indicate whether:

At this stage, the student:

  • Is attending as expected.
  • Demonstrates professional conduct.
  • Communicates appropriately.
  • Is engaging with placement activities.
  • Has no significant concerns regarding the student's successful completion of the placement.

The workplace supervisor also selects one of:

  • Satisfactory: Student is meeting professional expectations and placement continues.
  • Concern Identified: Minor or emerging concerns have been identified that require discussion and monitoring.
  • Unsatisfactory: Significant concerns have been identified regarding professional conduct, engagement, attendance, communication, or suitability for the placement. A Placement Review will be initiated.
  • Similarly, a short questionnaire may also be sent to the academic supervisor to confirm satisfactory commencement of the project and initial engagement with the student.

A rating of "Concern Identified" or "Unsatisfactory" will trigger a Placement Review process.

Process for concerns

If "Concern Identified" or "Unsatisfactory" is selected:

  1. The Course Coordinator contacts the student, workplace supervisor and academic supervisor.
  2. A review meeting is held, and an action plan is developed where appropriate. This meeting may typically involve:
  • Student
  • Workplace Supervisor
  • Academic Advisor (if assigned)
  • Course Coordinator

If appropriate and available, an Employability officer may also attend. Review should ideally take place during the week of 17th August if possible.

Following the review, the Course Coordinator may determine that:

  • the student continues in the placement without further action;
  • the student continues subject to a documented improvement plan;
  • the placement project is modified;
  • a new placement arrangement is required;
  • the student is advised to withdraw from the course prior to the census date (31 August 2026); or
  • if serious concerns cannot be resolved, the student may be removed from the placement and may be unable to satisfy course requirements, resulting in failure of the course.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Instructions and submission templates will be provided on Blackboard. Students are required to submit the Placement Commencement Check by the specified due date and ensure that workplace supervisor feedback is completed within the required timeframe.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: Assessments must be submitted on or before the due date.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
18/08/2026 4:00 pm

Submission documents must include a statement that the time and resources required for the project are appropriate. This is to ensure that there are adequate time and resources for the student to complete the project for their placement.
assessments.task_description:
The Project Proposal should outline the aims and scope of the project, the deliverables of the project, and the risks to successful completion of the project. In addition, submission documents must include a statement on the time and resources required for the project (see below).

A possible structure of the Project Proposal is:

  1. Title and supervisory team structure
  2. Introduction - briefly outline the background to the project and establish the context in which the proposed research is to be undertaken
  3. Goals - clearly identify the aim(s) of the project; this may be in the form of one or two major goals and sub-goals
  4. Methodology - identify the proposed steps that will be undertaken to complete the project
  5. Deliverables - if everything went according to the proposed plan, identify the expected deliverables from the project. 
  6. Project Scope, Time and Resource Statement - confirm that the project is appropriately scoped for completion within the placement period and that sufficient time, supervision, and resources are available to support successful completion of the project. Any limitations identified in relation to time, supervision, or resources should be discussed and subsequently reflected in the Risks section where relevant. (NEW)
  7. Risks - identify the risks to successful completion of the project
  8. References - list the papers/reports referenced in the Proposal. A bibliography may also be included (relevant papers/reports not referenced in the text).

The expected length of the Project Proposal is 10-15 pages. Writing in a clear, concise form is an important professional skill.

Please refer to Blackboard for a detailed marking criteria.

Project Scope, Time and Resource Statement (NEW)

As part of the project proposal, students must include a brief statement confirming that the proposed project is appropriately scoped for completion within the placement period and that sufficient time, supervision, and resources are available to support its successful completion. Resources may include access to facilities, equipment, software, data, materials, workplace support, and any other requirements necessary to undertake the project. The statement should demonstrate that the placement organisation has considered the project's requirements and will provide the student with adequate opportunity during the placement to undertake project-related activities necessary to meet the course learning objectives and assessment requirements. This includes sufficient time for project planning, investigation, analysis, meetings, and report preparation. The placement should support completion of an engineering project in a workplace setting, rather than solely providing general work experience.

Where limitations in time, supervision, or resources have been identified, students must outline these in the proposal and discuss them with their academic supervisor and workplace supervisor. The project scope, project plan, or placement arrangements may need to be revised to ensure the project can be completed successfully within the placement period.

AI

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Student to submit via Turnitin on the course Blackboard site.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
A combined Workplace Feedback Form and Student Reflection are to be completed at two points during the placement - mid-placement and end of placement.

This assessment will model a workplace performance review and will allow for direct oral feedback between the student and the workplace supervisor. 

This provides an opportunity to reflect on your development since the start of the placement and to write a reflective report.

Students should provide the feedback form to their supervisors at least two weeks prior to submission due date, and to schedule a meeting to discuss with their workplace supervisor at least a week before the due date.

More details on the submission will be available on Blackboard.

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Students to submit a single report (pdf format) including Workplace Feedback Form and Student reflection via Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Progress Review (Presentation and evidence of final report drafting; Oral defence Q&A)
assessments.due_date:
Meeting and Presentation with supervisory team 22/09/2026 - 6/10/2026

LAST day for Submission of required documentation at 4pm 6/10/2026 - 6/10/2026

A 2-week window has been provided for students to find a suitable time that is convenient for their advisory team. Students must seek to arrange this time with their advisors well in advance.

Required documentation is due on Tuesday, Week 10 16:00. (6/10/2026)
assessments.category: Paper/ Report/ Annotation, Placement, Presentation, Reflection
assessments.task_description:
The objective of this assessment is to evaluate your progress and ensure you are on track to meet the project's final deliverables. It also provides an opportunity for your supervisory team (including academic and industry supervisors) to give feedback and for you to reflect on it while discussing a plan moving forward.

In addition, this now includes a Question & Answer component to meet secure assessment requirements (NEW). 

The progress review should normally be conducted in person, with the student and at least one member of the review panel (e.g. an industry supervisor or academic supervisor) attending face-to-face. Other panel members may participate online where required. Exceptions to this requirement will be considered on a case-by-case basis by the Course Coordinator, taking into account individual circumstances and location constraints.

This assessment item consists of two components as outlined below:

1.     Progress Presentation

You will need to organise a one-hour meeting with your supervisory team within the dates outlined in the electronic course instance and deliver a 30-minute presentation outlining progress on your project. The presentation should include the following components:

  • Project background and objectives.
  • Summary of methodology.
  • Preliminary results, analysis and challenges.
  • Timeline and plans for remaining tasks.

In this presentation, you will highlight the milestones achieved so far, identify any challenges or hurdles encountered, and discuss strategies to address them. Additionally, you will justify any changes made to the project plan, if applicable. Following the presentation, the supervisory team will provide feedback on progress, offering suggestions for improvement, as well as guidance for the successful completion of the project and final report.


2.     Evidence of final report drafting process

During this meeting you will also need to demonstrate that you have begun writing your final report with evidence of a draft of the initial chapters or sections of your report, this may include but not limited to an introductory chapter, where the research problem, objectives, and significance are introduced, and a review or technical background or prior art where applicable.

It is recommended to submit this draft report to supervisors prior to the meeting to give them sufficient time to review (e.g. 1 week) and provide some feedback during the meeting. Please discuss with your supervisors when they wish to receive the draft.


3.     Oral Defence / Interview - Question and Answer (NEW)

The panel (which must include an academic supervisor) must ask critical questions about the project, presentation, progress, and report. This component is designed to confirm the student's understanding of the project, the work undertaken, the methodologies used, the interpretation of results, and the drafting of the final report. It also provides an opportunity for the student to demonstrate their individual contribution, critical thinking, and ability to justify decisions and respond to feedback.

The question-and-answer session will normally occur immediately following the progress presentation and discussion of the draft report. The duration of the session may vary depending on the nature and complexity of the project and the issues raised by the panel. Students should be prepared to answer questions related to the project objectives, methods, outcomes achieved to date, challenges encountered, future work plans, and the content of their draft report. Performance in this component will contribute to the overall assessment and forms part of the University's secure assessment requirements.

Students must satisfactorily demonstrate authorship of, and understanding of, the project work during the oral defence interview component to pass this assessment task.


Submission requirements:

You are required to submit a single PDF document with your name and student number by the due date specified in the course instance that includes the following:

  • A copy of your presentation
  • A summary of feedback provided by the supervisory team with a short reflection on the feedback and how this will inform your actions moving forward
  • A draft structure of your final report

Please refer to Blackboard for a detailed marking criteria.

This assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT). Students are advised that the use of AI or MT technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct
assessments.submission_guidelines: Student to submit via Turnitin on the course Blackboard site.

assessments.assessment_title: BEME Placement Symposium - Oral Presentation and participation
assessments.due_date:
Recommended timeframe for draft presentation review by Industry Supervisor (not submitted to UQ) 19/11/2026 - 26/11/2026

Oral Presentation / Symposium 1/12/2026 - 16/12/2026

Presentation Approval

Students must provide a draft presentation to their industry supervisor for review and approval prior to presenting. This review process is intended to ensure that any confidential, commercially sensitive, or proprietary information is identified and removed or appropriately managed before the presentation is delivered.

To allow sufficient time for review and feedback, students are encouraged to submit their draft presentation approximately three weeks (e.g. 19/11/2026) before the scheduled presentation date.

Written approval from the industry supervisor (e.g. email confirmation) must be obtained before the presentation is delivered. Students should retain a copy of this approval, as it may be requested by the Course Coordinator. Submission of the approval is not required unless specifically requested.

Presentation Schedule

The date and time of presentations will be advised during the semester and will fall within the assessment period specified above.

The ENGG7292 symposium is currently anticipated to be held on Thursday, 10 December 2026, approximately two days after submission of the final thesis/report. This date is indicative only and remains subject to confirmation.

Presentation Slides

While there is no formal submission requirement for presentation slides, the Course Coordinator may request a copy of the industry-approved presentation before or after the presentation. Requests made before the presentation would typically occur no more than one day in advance.
assessments.task_description:
Students are expected to attend and participate in the BEME Placement Symposium for two sessions (approximately 3 hours). This provides an opportunity for students to develop their communication skills, learn from the experiences of others, and think critically about engineering projects and professional practice through active engagement and by asking relevant questions.

Students are expected to present a non-confidential overview of their work and participate actively in the symposium by listening to and asking thoughtful questions of their peers.

Students may be requested to contribute to the assessment of other students' presentations - this should be done professionally and meaningfully.

Students may need to adapt their work so it is suitable to present to a public audience, and should only present results from their thesis that their industry advisors have permitted.

Students are expected to present their work to peers, academic staff, and placement supervisors on campus. Online presentations may be approved where attendance on campus is not reasonably possible. Students should discuss and review content of presentations in order to avoid disclosing confidential information.

Students should aim for a presentation of no more than 11-12 minutes (e.g. 12 slides), allowing approximately three minutes for questions within the allocated 15 minute presentation slot.

A suggested format for the presentation is as follows:

  • Title
  • Introduction: Context and Objectives of the Placement
  • Technical Background: Literature Review and/or State of the Art
  • Project Methodology and Management: Technical Approach, Resources, Scope and Methods
  • Project Outcomes and Results
  • Conclusions and Recommendations
  • Acknowledgements

Please refer to Blackboard for a detailed marking criteria.

Students should be prepared to respond to questions from the audience regarding their project and presentation.

Additional notes:

This task is about communication of research and project work to a broader audience, which is independent of the assessment on the report. Students are encouraged to develop and present a "public-facing" version of their work (i.e. non-confidential components) and to obtain approval from their workplace supervisor. They should discuss this with their supervisor well in advance of presentation day to determine what aspects can be presented publicly. All presentations should be reviewed by workplace supervisor prior to presenting to ensure no confidential information is presented. In most circumstances, it is anticipated that presentations can be adequately adjusted to allow presentation publicly although it may mean a student can't present everything they have done.

If the workplace supervisor determines that a suitable public-facing presentation cannot be prepared without disclosing confidential information, they may contact the coordinator explaining the situation and request permission to present to a select set of academic staff and workplace members only. This should be done well in advance of the scheduling process for presentations. The coordinator may seek further information from the workplace and/or academic supervisor. Students are still required to attend the symposium.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

assessments.task_description:
At the conclusion of the placement the student will submit a Final Report or thesis documenting the progress and outcomes of the investigation. This report should serve as a professional record of the project and its outcomes for both UQ and the placement host organisation. Details on formatting and submission will be available on Blackboard. 

Early Submission: If you are currently taking ENGG7292 and this is your final semester, there is currently no provision for the early submission of assessment before the end of academic semester in October.

Artificial Intelligence and Machine Translation tools: This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies to a limited extent, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.

Additional information:

The report/thesis format is flexible and should be adapted to suit the nature of the project. One possible structure is provided below:

  • Title Page
  • Cover Letter – confidential or non-confidential, including a statement of originality (refer to templates on Blackboard)
  • Executive Summary / Abstract – concise 1 page summary of project outcomes (could be read as a standalone document)
  • Acknowledgements
  • Table of Contents
  • List of Figures
  • List of Tables
  • Introduction – placement context and business/academic driver for the project
  • Technical Background – review of foundational principles, relevant literature, and/or state-of-the-art
  • Project Description – aim, objectives, scope, methodology, and deliverables
  • Results and Analysis – critical summary of project findings
  • Conclusions and Recommendations of future work
  • References
  • Appendix A – summary of project timeline, risk analysis, and opportunities
  • Appendix B – As required

The main body of the report would not normally be expected to exceed 50 pages (excluding appendices).

The structure provided above is intended as guidance only and is not mandatory. Students should adopt a report structure that best suits the nature and objectives of their project.
